title: Pablo Hidalgo
content:
{{Top|rwp|sprot}}
{{Youmay|the real-world [[Author/Legends|author]]|[[Palob Godalhi]] or the [[Rodian]] [[Pablo]]}}
{{Person
|image=[[File:Pablo_Hidalgo_question.jpg]]
|name=Pablo Hidalgo
|birth=[[Wikipedia:Santiago|Santiago]], [[Wikipedia:Chile|Chile]]<ref name="Korpil">{{WebCite|url=http://korpil.net/2010/11/interview-with-pablo-hidalgo|text=Interview with Pablo Hidalgo|date=2010-11-24|work=[https://web.archive.org/web/20101229143644/http://korpil.net Datapad de Korpil]|archivedate=20101204221129|nolive=1}}</ref>
|death=
|listedpronouns=<!--Only include pronouns if they have been explicitly specified by the individual to which this article pertains.-->
|nationality=
|occupation=*Senior Creative Executive, Franchise & Story<ref name="Kenobi doc">''[[Obi-Wan Kenobi: A Jedi's Return]]''</ref> {{C|formerly}}<ref name="Pablo Jobs">{{Twitter|pabl0hidalgo|status/1819189854179414227|Pablo Hidalgo|date=2024-08-01|quote=Hey @WookOfficial I am not currently a "creative executive," and the IPDG hasn't existed in years. :)|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/CGAc4}}</ref>
*''Star Wars'' Lore Advisor<ref name="Part I">{{Kenobi|Part I}}</ref>
|companies=[[Lucasfilm]]<ref name="Part I" />
|website=
}}
{{Quote|The job is mostly research into precedent and history. I'm not the one who says "how about a dog that plays basketball?" I'm one of a team that says "there's nothing in the rulebook that says a dog can't play basketball."|Pablo Hidalgo on his role as Lore Advisor|<ref name="Pablo Air Bud reference">{{Twitter|pabl0hidalgo|status/1532479211683713026|Pablo Hidalgo|quote=The job is mostly research into precedent and history. I'm not the one who says "how about a dog that plays basketball?" I'm one of a team that says "there's nothing in the rulebook that says a dog can't play basketball."|date=2022-06-02|archivedate=20220602234549}}</ref>}}
'''Pablo Hidalgo'''  is an author at [[Lucasfilm]]. He has been identified as a Senior Creative Executive Franchise Story figure<ref name="Kenobi doc" /> and member of the [[Lucasfilm Story Group]].<ref name="SW Show Interview">{{StarWarsShow|GvT2F1j-SvA|Pablo Hidalgo Extended Interview}}</ref> He was often referred to as the ''Star Wars'' Lore Advisor by the time of the release of [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi|''Obi-Wan Kenobi'']].<ref name="Part I" />

In addition to his ''[[Star Wars]]'' work, Hidalgo has written or co-authored reference guides for both the ''[[Wikipedia:G.I. Joe|G.I. Joe]]'' and ''[[Star Wars Transformers|Transformers]]'' franchises.<ref name="awakensvisual">''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref>

==Biography==
{{Quote|Oh boy. Please writers, don't oversell it! I've only *just* managed to convince people I didn't write and direct these things. I'm just a Clippy that appears at the bottom of a script in progress. "It looks like you're trying to mention an Outer Rim planet... would you like some help?"|Pablo Hidalgo on his role as Lore Advisor upon being called "the guardian of canon" in an article|<ref name="Pablo Clippy">{{Twitter|pabl0hidalgo|status/1533188585855123456|Pablo Hidalgo|date=2022-06-04|archivedate=20220604204708}}</ref>}}
Pablo Hidalgo is known for his creation of material ranging from [[West End Games]] role-playing game (RPG) resources to content for [[StarWars.com]], where he went under the screen name of '''Pabawan'''. He is also a founding member of the [http://www.myuselessknowledge.com/swfa Star Wars Fanboy Association]. He served as Internet Content Manager for [[Lucas Online]] until 2011, and also worked as a comics writer on StarWars.com. Until January 2000, Hidalgo wrote and managed the Star Wars Index at echostation.com, when he abandoned the project to work for [[Lucasfilm]]. The site included some of his original ''Star Wars'' artwork.<ref name="Index">{{WebCite|url=http://index.echostation.com|text=The Star Wars Index|work=[https://web.archive.org/web/20000229132139/http://www.echostation.com Echo Station]|archivedate=20071014193742|nolive=1}}</ref>

[[File:PablosAwesomeHatInROTSHD.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Pablo Hidalgo's cameo in the film Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith.]]
He had a cameo playing [[Janu Godalhi]] (an anagram of Pablo's real-life father, Juan Hidalgo) in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']] and was also referenced with the character [[Palob Godalhi/Legends|Palob Godalhi]]. After [[The Walt Disney Company]] acquired Lucasfilm, Hidalgo was assigned a job within the<ref name="Art Last Jedi">''[[The Art of Star Wars: The Last Jedi]]''</ref> still newly formed<ref name="ForceCast">{{WebCite|url=http://www.forcecast.net/story/home/ForceCast_273_The_Galaxy_Is_Reading_154431.asp|text=ForceCast #273: The Galaxy is Reading|work=[[The ForceCast]]|archivedate=20200412073227}}</ref> [[Lucasfilm Story Group]], a small group of ''[[Star Wars]]'' experts whose main purpose is to create and maintain one cohesive canon, thereby eliminating the previous hierarchical canon.<ref name="Art Last Jedi" /> Hidalgo was also<ref name="Pablo Jobs" /> a creative executive in story development at Lucasfilm, as well as one of the members of the [[Intellectual Property Development Group]].<ref name="Art Last Jedi" /> He is well known for writing the reference books that accompany the release of new movies.<ref name="TROS VD">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> Hidalgo has stated he does not work in the production side of Lucasfilm. There are only minor exceptions to that rule, including minor parts of ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' that can be credited to Hidalgo and other minor exceptions<ref name="Not Same">{{Bluesky|username=infinata.bsky.social|url=post/3llxfek7hnc2p|name=Pablo Hidalgo|date=2025-04-03|quote=Saw this come up as a response to a different post, but aside from some minor elements in Rebels, there's very little that I do that ends up on screen. I do not work in production. 🙏|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/bqZrA}}</ref> like background details.<ref name="Gack Written">{{Bluesky|username=infinata.bsky.social|url=post/3llxfj2zfrk2p|name=Pablo Hidalgo|date=2025-04-03|quote=(Unless you consider gack written on a screen significant, which I suppose I recognize an online group might think so)|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/HTYAI}}</ref> Hidalgo appeared as a recurring guest on [[Rebels Recon]] from 2014 to 2018, a behind-the-scenes YouTube series produced by [[StarWars.com]] examining each Star Wars Rebels episode, where he answers fan questions about Star Wars lore relayed by [[Andy Gutierrez]], who also appeared on the show.<ref name="Rebels Recon S2 Ep15">{{RebelsRecon|QeSPdY01XpA|#2.15: Inside "Homecoming" | Star Wars Rebels|sw_url=video/rebels-recon-inside-homecoming}}</ref>

Hidalgo served as the ''Star Wars'' lore advisor for the [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan Kenobi|''Obi-Wan Kenobi'']] television series.<ref name="Part I" /> During the writing process, writer [[Joby Harold]] thought of having [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] learn that [[Anakin Skywalker]] survived their [[duel on Mustafar]] to get the series to a place of intense drama for Kenobi. He first had to ask Hidalgo how much Kenobi knows during the show's time period, and Harold was given permission to use the scene.<ref name="Harold justified">{{WebCite|url=https://www.thewrap.com/why-obi-wan-didnt-know-anakin-was-alive-vader-joby-harold|text=How 'Obi-Wan Kenobi' Writer Justified Obi-Wan Not Knowing Anakin Was Alive|author=Adam Chitwood|date=2022-06-02|work=[[Wikipedia:TheWrap|''The Wrap'']]|archivedate=20220602152919}}</ref> Hidalgo provided many resources for propmaster [[Brad Elliott]] and his team.<ref name="Weapons of Inquis">{{SW|url=news/inside-the-lucasfilm-archive-obi-wan-kenobi-props-2|text=Inside the Lucasfilm Archive: Weapons of the Inquisitorius from the ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' Limited Series}}</ref> Hidalgo and Elliott discussed the look of [[wupiupi]] [[coin]]s and their proper conversion rate with [[trugut]]s and [[Galactic Credit Standard|credits]]. This allowed the two to figure out the [[Currency|cost]] of some common goods in ''Star Wars'' and a minimum wage for the [[Tibidon Station|whale-carving station]] that [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] worked at. It was decided that a full shift paid one trugut and three wupiupi.<ref name="Elegant weapon">{{SW|url=news/inside-the-lucasfilm-archive-obi-wan-kenobi-props-1|text=Inside the Lucasfilm Archive: An Elegant Weapon and Other Jedi Artifacts from the ''Obi-Wan Kenobi'' Limited Series}}</ref> Hidalgo pointed to information originally established by ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' for use in ''[[Star Wars: Andor]]''<ref name="What's This">{{Bluesky|username=infinata.bsky.social|url=post/3lld42pc2uc2b|name=Pablo Hidalgo|date=2025-03-26|quote=* whistles innocently * oh what's this? Sounds interesting. Who would cause such a thing to happen?|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/43Xu2}}</ref> to pay tribute to a game series that helped generate his love for the ''Star Wars'' universe.<ref name="Perfect Age">{{Bluesky|username=infinata.bsky.social|url=post/3lld6uqa3w22b|name=Pablo Hidalgo|date=2025-03-26|quote=The WEG game hit me at the perfect age and let me make my own Star Wars universe. Always happy to bring some of that material forward when I can.|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/cmAzT}}</ref>

In an interview in early [[2026]], President of Lucasfilm, [[Kathleen Kennedy]], described Hidalgo and Chief Creative Officer and future Co-President, [[Dave Filoni]], as being "like the walking encyclopedias inside the company. They always have the answer when you ask, 'Hey, can I do this with a lightsaber?' Or, 'What colors were the sabers?' Or anything else you need to know with what has come before".<ref name="Exit interview">{{WebCite|url=https://deadline.com/2026/01/kathleen-kennedy-exit-interview-1236665253/|text=A Shift In The Force For Lucasfilm’s Kathleen Kennedy: The Exit Interview|author=Mike Flemming Jr.|date=2026-01-15|work=[[Wikipedia:Deadline Hollywood|''Deadline'']]|archivedate=20260115233219}}</ref>
